reason: Network outage affecting 120+ users required L3 architecture review to identify failed core switch module
notes: Root cause was a failed line card on the Building B distribution switch. Replaced and restored service.

reason: VPN disconnect pattern pointed to a wireless controller configuration issue beyond L1 scope
notes: Wireless controller had conflicting DTLS settings causing VPN tunnel instability. Updated controller config.

reason: Jenkins memory leak required L3 investigation into JVM tuning and plugin compatibility
notes: Identified outdated pipeline plugin causing memory leak. Updated plugin and increased JVM heap allocation.

reason: Split-tunnel policy changes required network architecture review and firewall rule updates
notes: Updated VPN group policy for contractor OU to enable split-tunnel. Verified with three contractor test accounts.

reason: Account was in a locked state that self-service and L1 tools could not resolve. Required AD admin access.
notes: Account had stale Kerberos tokens from pre-leave sessions. Cleared tokens and reset credentials at AD level.

reason: Firewall rule rollback requires change advisory board approval and L3 network architecture oversight due to production impact
notes: Emergency CAB convened. Identifying which of the 47 new rules is blocking payment API endpoints.

reason: Site-to-site VPN tunnel instability requires coordination with AWS support and ISP. L3 architecture engagement needed.
notes: AWS support case opened. ISP investigating possible BGP route flapping on their end.

reason: SCCM patch deployment failure pattern suggests Group Policy conflict. Needs L3 review of endpoint security stack interactions.
notes: Error 0x80070005 indicates access denied. Investigating whether CrowdStrike Falcon is blocking the SCCM agent.
